context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after enabling host-proxied LLM streaming end-to-end. The streaming lane moved from gated/disabled to opt-in live host-proxied provider SSE. Architecture remains: generic SSE framing/reader in tractor::streaming; LLM/provider JSON and AgentResponse projection in Tractor llm_stream_events; provider credentials and route enforcement stay host-owned.
current_tasks: New commits after compaction: ed6858a3 test(tractor): prove stream chunks persist before close; 1772661a feat(agent): enable host-proxied provider streaming; 3750ea61 test(agent): cover stream opt-in body gate; 68544fbd docs(agent): mark host streaming enabled; e39bc39b feat(tractor): synthesize streamed tool calls; 276db196 test(agent): cover streamed tool call round trip; 09f47814 docs(agent): note streamed tool call support.

Provider streaming is now opt-in live: packages/pi-agent/src/streaming_config.rs has streaming_reader_available() -> true, but provider stream:true still requires LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES truthy. Missing env keeps buffered behavior.

End-to-end ignored harness coverage exists in packages/tractor/tests/pi_agent_harness.rs: harness_streaming_opt_in_stores_partials_and_final_response proves LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES=1 sends provider stream:true, stores partial AgentResponse chunks, and stores final response at last partial sequence+1. harness_streaming_tool_call_round_trip_still_completes proves streamed OpenAI tool_call deltas synthesize final JSON, execute bash tool, make a second streamed request, and store final tool-call log.

Host live-read proof: complete_http_stream_stores_first_chunk_before_response_closes uses a chunked fake SSE server to prove the first partial AgentResponse is persisted before the provider response closes.

Tractor stream final assembly now handles text plus streamed tool semantics. OpenAI-compatible tool_calls deltas are accumulated by index/id/name/arguments into final choices[0].message.tool_calls. Anthropic content_block_start/tool_use and input_json_delta partial_json are accumulated into final content tool_use blocks. Text deltas still become partial AgentResponse nodes.
ADR-053 is marked Accepted and specs/ADRs/README.md notes initial host-proxied text and tool-call streaming implemented. ROADMAP marks host-proxied streaming enabled and end-to-end harness proven.

Validations after latest code/docs: pi-agent wasm cargo check; pi-agent streaming_config tests; pi-agent provider_runtime_stream_body_gate tests; tractor cargo check; tractor read_sse_data_events_limited tests; complete_http_stream tests; store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader tests; synthesize/tool-call stream tests; ignored pi_agent_harness streaming text and streaming tool tests; npm run project:validate; git diff --check; npm run clean:light.
blockers: No GitHub Actions run yet for the long local stack; remote origin/develop remains behind all streaming commits.
Disk remains tight: after clean:light ~11GB free, Rust targets ~5.1GB.
Full tractor-ts unit suite remains noisy locally due missing WASM fixtures; avoid broad TS suite unless addressing fixtures.
pi-lens/LSP monitor continued to crash on large Rust/markdown edits with memory access out of bounds, but cargo checks/tests passed.
Streaming is opt-in only via LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES; default behavior remains buffered/non-streaming.
next_actions: If ready to share, push the stack and watch CI using gh run watch --exit-status (do not manually poll). Before push, inspect git log origin/develop..HEAD and git status --short.
Consider adding a small user-facing doc note for LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES now that it is live opt-in, if README/CLI docs expose provider env flags.

Consider reducing duplicated provider stream parsing between pi-agent provider_runtime/stream_events.rs and Tractor llm_stream_events.rs by extracting shared target-neutral LLM SSE parsing, but only if worthwhile and atomic.
Consider a follow-up harness for Anthropic-style streamed tool_use deltas if an Anthropic harness path exists or can be cheaply added without credentials.

Client/UI reassembly remains a consumer concern: partial AgentResponse.content chunks are deltas and final AgentResponse is full content. Verify WebSocket clients handle is_final/sequence correctly before claiming polished UX.
open_questions: Should direct AgentResponse partial storage remain the long-term host projection, or should a generic StreamChunk observation type be introduced and projected to AgentResponse?
Should final synthesized usage stay zero for streamed responses until provider usage deltas are parsed, or should usage be accumulated from provider stream usage events where available?
Should LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES be exposed in formal CLI/user docs as experimental opt-in?
Should Anthropic streaming tool_use have a dedicated end-to-end harness, or are unit tests enough for now?
When should the large local stack be pushed to origin/develop and CI watched?
key_decisions_pending: Long-term StreamChunk vs direct AgentResponse partials.
Shared parser extraction vs current duplicated parser logic.
Usage accounting semantics for provider SSE streams.
Whether to push current large stack now.
Client reassembly/UX criteria for declaring streaming polished.
